	{"bbox_2d": [167, 161, 920, 233], "label": "The STUSB4500 is a USB Type-C™ and power delivery controller IC for sink applications. It is able to negotiate a power delivery contract with a source without MCU support (auto-run mode). It relies on proprietary algorithms and configurable PDO (power data objects) thanks to an integrated non-volatile memory. It supports dead battery mode to allow a system to be powered from an external source directly. Combined with its capability to negotiate directly a power contract, the STUSB4500 is the ideal controller device for autonomous systems requiring high power charging profile to be fully operational."},
	{"bbox_2d": [167, 241, 393, 253], "label": "The STUSB4500 major role is to:"},
	{"bbox_2d": [167, 256, 692, 268], "label": "1. Detect the connection between two USB Type-C ports (attach detection)"},
	{"bbox_2d": [167, 272, 492, 284], "label": "2. Establish a valid source-to-sink connection"},
	{"bbox_2d": [167, 288, 560, 300], "label": "3. Determine the attached device mode: source or debug accessory"},
	{"bbox_2d": [167, 304, 828, 316], "label": "4. Resolve cable orientation and twist connections to establish USB data routing (MUX control)"},
	{"bbox_2d": [167, 320, 715, 332], "label": "5. Negotiate a USB power delivery (PD) contract with a PD capable source device"},
	{"bbox_2d": [167, 336, 720, 348], "label": "6. Configure the incoming VBUS power path and the charging paths accordingly"},
	{"bbox_2d": [167, 352, 681, 364], "label": "7. Monitor the VBUS power path and manage the VBUS voltage transitions"},
	{"bbox_2d": [167, 368, 449, 380], "label": "8. Handle the high voltage protections"},
	{"bbox_2d": [167, 388, 389, 400], "label": "The STUSB4500 also provides:"},
